1: Thinly slice onion. Melt herb butter in a pan over medium-low and cook onion 12–15 min until deeply caramelized; set aside.
In the same pan, toast arborio rice 1 min. Add broth concentrate dissolved in 3 cups hot water, one ladle at a time, stirring until absorbed before adding more (~20 min) until creamy.
Season steak strips with salt; sear in a hot pan 2–3 min until browned.
Stir parmesan and half the onions into the risotto. Fold in steak.
Top with remaining onions, arugula tossed in balsamic, blue cheese, and chives.
2: Dice onion and poblano. Cook in oil over medium 5 min until soft.
Add diced chicken and chile-cumin rub; cook 5 min until chicken is no longer pink.
Stir in cream base, broth concentrate, corn, and 2 cups water. Simmer 10 min.
Finish with a squeeze of lime and season to taste.
Ladle into bowls; top with mozzarella and tortilla strips.
3: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil and Italian seasoning, roast 20 min. Add broccolini for the last 10 min.
Pat mahi-mahi dry, season with salt and red pepper flakes.
Sear fish in oil 3–4 min per side until it flakes easily.
Warm cream cheese, herb butter, and broth concentrate with a splash of water to make a sauce.
Plate fish and vegetables, spoon over herb cream, top with crispy onions.
4: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil and sesame oil, roast 25–30 min, tossing once.
Mix mayo with gochujang for spicy mayo. Toss cabbage with rice vinegar for slaw.
Sear steak strips in a hot pan 2–3 min, then toss with teriyaki glaze until coated.
Toast buns. Spread with spicy mayo.
Build sandwiches with steak and slaw. Sprinkle fries with sesame seeds.
5: Toss panko with a little oil and toast in a dry pan until golden; set aside.
Season chicken with garlic pepper. Brush with dijon, press on toasted panko, and sear 4–5 min per side until cooked through.
Chop romaine, halve tomatoes, and combine with red cabbage in a bowl.
Whisk mayo and dijon into the honey-dijon dressing; toss with the salad.
Slice chicken over the salad; top with candied walnuts and parmesan.
6: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss broccoli with oil and roast 18–20 min.
Mix cream cheese, cheddar, spinach, parmesan, and ranch seasoning.
Cut a pocket in each chicken breast; stuff with the cheese mixture.
Season chicken with garlic pepper, sear 3 min per side, then bake 12–15 min to 165°F.
Drizzle with lemon garlic butter; top with crispy onions. Serve with broccoli.
7: Char corn and diced poblano in a dry hot pan until blistered; set aside.
Season steak strips with taco seasoning and sear 2–3 min.
Warm tortillas and crisp them if desired.
Mix sour cream with lemon juice and a pinch of salt.
Layer tortillas with steak, corn-poblano, cheese, crema, and cilantro.
8: Preheat oven to 400°F. Peel and cut carrots; toss with oil, roast 25 min.
Season chicken with savory seasoning and herbes de Provence; sear 4 min per side, then finish in oven 10 min to 165°F.
Melt butter with brown sugar and demi-glace; toss roasted carrots to glaze.
Toast pecans briefly in a dry pan.
Plate chicken and carrots; scatter pecans and chives.
9: Preheat oven to 400°F. Halve Brussels sprouts, toss with oil, roast 20–25 min.
Season chicken with smoked maple; sear until browned. Sauté mushrooms; deglaze with marsala, add broth and cream cheese to make sauce.
Roll out puff pastry; wrap each chicken breast with a spoon of mushrooms and parmesan.
Bake pastry 18–20 min until golden and chicken reaches 165°F.
Serve sliced with marsala mushroom sauce and Brussels sprouts.
10: Cook rice per package (bring rice + 1 cup water to boil, cover, simmer 15 min).
Dice mango and cucumber; mix with minced jalapeño, green onion, and lime juice for salsa.
Season salmon with fiesta seasoning and a pinch of salt.
Sear salmon skin-side down 4 min, flip, brush with sweet chili sauce, cook 3 more min.
Serve salmon over rice, topped with mango salsa.
11: Season steak strips with a pinch of salt; sear in a hot pan 2 min until browned. Remove.
Add matchstick carrots and green onion whites; stir-fry 1 min.
Add cooked spaghetti, teriyaki glaze, sambal, and sugar; toss to coat and heat through.
Return steak; add lime juice and toss.
Top with peanuts and green onion tops.
12: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with potato spice and oil, roast 25 min; finish with parmesan.
Halve Brussels sprouts, roast alongside 20 min.
Warm cream cheese with spinach and artichoke, a splash of water, and lemon-herb seasoning into a creamy sauce.
Season sirloin with steak seasoning; sear 3–4 min per side to desired doneness, rest 5 min.
Plate steak topped with spinach-artichoke cream; serve with potatoes and sprouts.
13: Cook orzo in salted water until tender, ~9 min; drain.
Halve tomatoes. Sear shrimp seasoned with garlic salt and red pepper flakes 1–2 min per side; remove.
In the same pan, wilt spinach; add tomatoes and herb butter.
Stir in orzo, crème fraîche, and parmesan to make it creamy.
Fold in shrimp; top with feta.
14: Dice pineapple, mince shallot and jalapeño; combine with corn and lime juice for salsa.
Season steak strips with adobo and chile-cumin; sear 2–3 min.
Melt chipotle butter and toss with steak.
Serve steak over rice, topped with pineapple salsa.
15: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with garlic-and-parsley seasoning and oil, roast 25–30 min.
Mix parmesan and cream cheese with a splash of water into a spread.
Form beef into 2 patties, season with garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side.
Toast buns; spread with parmesan cream and balsamic fig glaze.
Build burgers with arugula and blue cheese; garnish fries with parsley.
16: Toast pearl couscous in oil 1 min, add 1.5 cups water and broth concentrate, simmer 12 min until tender.
Dice bell pepper and tomato. Sear shrimp seasoned with chimichurri 1–2 min per side; remove.
Stir couscous, vegetables, pesto, and green onion together.
Fold in shrimp; finish with lemon and parsley.
17: Preheat oven to 400°F. Trim green beans, toss with oil.
Sauté shallot; add cream cheese, white cheddar, flour, broth, and water to make a sauce. Toss half with green beans and roast 15 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt; sear until browned.
Wrap each breast in puff pastry; bake 18–20 min until golden and 165°F.
Serve sliced with shallot cream sauce and cheesy green beans.
18: Boil red potatoes until tender, ~15 min; mash with butter and a splash of cream base.
Peel and cut carrots; roast or steam with a drizzle of honey until tender.
Sear fish in oil 3–4 min per side until flaky. In the same pan, warm remaining cream base with minced garlic and lemon.
Plate fish over mash with carrots; spoon garlic cream sauce over, top with chives.
19: Whisk tempura mix with cold water; coat shrimp, then press in panko.
Fry shrimp in shallow oil until golden, ~2 min per side; drain.
Stir-fry snow peas and garlic 2 min; add garlic sesame sauce and sriracha to glaze.
Serve shrimp over rice with snow peas; sprinkle sesame seeds.
20: Thinly slice cucumber; toss with slaw mix, rice vinegar, and a pinch of salt for the slaw.
Sear steak strips seasoned with a little pho broth concentrate 2–3 min.
Split and toast French rolls; spread with sriracha mayo.
Fill rolls with steak, slaw, sliced jalapeño, and cilantro.
21: Preheat oven to 425°F. Make thin crosswise cuts across each potato (hasselback); brush with oil and garlic salt, bake 40–45 min.
Toss asparagus with oil; roast last 12 min.
Season sirloin with garlic salt and black pepper; sear 3–4 min per side, rest.
Warm cream base with dijon and coarse pepper to make sauce.
Plate steak with pepper cream, hasselback potatoes topped with parmesan and herb butter, and asparagus with chives.
22: Slice onion; sauté until soft. Season steak strips with taco seasoning and sear 2–3 min.
Dice tomato and mix with lime juice and cilantro for pico.
Fill tortillas with steak, onion, and cheddar-jack; fold into quesadillas.
Cook quesadillas in a dry pan 2 min per side until crisp and melty.
Serve with sour cream and pico de gallo.
23: Char corn in a dry pan; dice tomato.
Season diced chicken with chipotle seasoning; cook 6–7 min until done.
Toss chicken with BBQ sauce and lime juice.
Fill cups with chicken, corn, tomato, pepper jack, and cilantro.
24: Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil potatoes until tender; mash with butter and a splash of milk.
Toss asparagus with oil; roast 12 min.
Season sirloin with steak seasoning; sear 3–4 min per side, rest.
Plate steak topped with pear, shallot, and blue cheese; serve with mash, asparagus, and chives.
25: Dice cucumber and tomato; mix a little into tzatziki if desired.
Season beef with gyro seasoning and garlic salt; cook, breaking apart, 6–8 min until browned.
Fill pita with beef, cucumber, tomato, onion, tzatziki, and feta.
26: Season pulled pork with chipotle seasoning; warm in a pan with BBQ sauce.
Fill tortillas with pork and cheddar-jack; roll tightly into flautas.
Cook flautas seam-side down in oil, turning, until crisp all over.
Serve flautas with salsa, sour cream, and cilantro.
27: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss broccoli with oil, roast 18–20 min.
Layer ham and swiss onto each chicken breast, fold over. Dip in egg, coat in panko.
Sear chicken in oil until golden, then bake 12–15 min to 165°F.
Warm butter and smoked paprika; drizzle over chicken.
Serve with broccoli and honey dijon for dipping.
28: Preheat oven to 400°F. Dice poblano; char corn.
Cook beef with taco seasoning until browned; stir in poblano, corn, broth, and a splash of water.
Whisk cornmeal with water, melted butter, and garlic salt into a batter.
Spread beef in a baking dish, top with cornmeal batter and cheddar-jack.
Bake 20–25 min until the top is set and golden.
29: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add green beans last 12 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt and minced rosemary; sear 4 min per side, finish in oven to 165°F.
Sauté shallot; add cream base, broth concentrate, and herb butter to make sauce.
Plate chicken with rosemary cream sauce, potatoes, and green beans; top with chives.
30: Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil potatoes; mash with butter and a splash of cream base.
Slice mushrooms; sauté with shallot until golden.
Deglaze with white wine, add remaining cream base and sage; simmer into a sauce.
Season sirloin with steak seasoning and garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side, rest.
Plate steak over mash, spoon mushroom-sage sauce over.
31: Cook linguine in salted water until al dente; reserve a little pasta water.
Halve tomato. Sear shrimp seasoned with garlic salt 1–2 min per side; remove.
Sauté garlic; add cream base, lemon, and parmesan to make alfredo.
Toss linguine and tomatoes in the sauce, loosening with pasta water. Fold in shrimp.
Butter French bread, sprinkle with garlic salt and Italian seasoning, toast; serve alongside.
32: Slice onion; sauté until soft. Season steak strips with taco seasoning and garlic salt; sear 2–3 min.
Fill tortillas with steak, onion, and nacho cheese blend; roll into flautas.
Cook flautas seam-side down in oil, turning until crisp.
Serve with salsa and sour cream.
33: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil, roast 25–30 min.
Form beef into 2 patties; season generously with smoky peppercorn seasoning and garlic salt.
Sear patties 3–4 min per side; add cheddar to melt.
Thinly slice onion; toast buns and spread with mayo.
Build burgers with lettuce and onion; serve with fries.
34: Season steak strips with taco seasoning; sear 2–3 min.
Dice tomato; mix with lime juice and cilantro for pico.
Fill tortillas with steak and nacho cheese; fold into quesadillas.
Cook 2 min per side until crisp.
Serve with guacamole and pico de gallo.
35: Preheat oven to 400°F. Trim green beans, toss with oil, roast 15 min.
Slice mushrooms; sauté with shallot. Add cream base, flour, and thyme to make sauce.
Season chicken with garlic salt; sear until browned.
Wrap each breast in puff pastry; bake 18–20 min until golden and 165°F.
Serve sliced with mushroom cream sauce and green beans.
36: Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil potatoes; mash with truffle butter and a splash of cream base until smooth.
Toss asparagus with oil; roast 12 min.
Season sirloin with steak seasoning and garlic salt.
Sear steak 3–4 min per side to desired doneness; rest 5 min.
Plate steak with truffle mash and asparagus; top with parmesan and chives.
37: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add green beans last 12 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt and smoked paprika. Coat in panko.
Sear chicken in shallow oil 4–5 min per side until golden and cooked through.
Mix mayo with lemon juice for a quick sauce.
Serve schnitzel with potatoes, green beans, lemon wedges, and the sauce.
38: Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil potatoes; mash with butter and a splash of cream base.
Roast or steam green beans until tender.
Coat sirloin in flour, then panko seasoned with garlic salt and pepper; fry in shallow oil until golden.
Make gravy: whisk remaining flour into pan drippings, add cream base and water, simmer.
Plate steak over mash with gravy; serve green beans alongside.
39: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil, roast 25–30 min.
Whisk flour with water into a batter; coat yellowtail, then press in panko seasoned with old bay and garlic salt.
Fry fish in shallow oil 3–4 min per side until golden and flaky.
Mix mayo with malt vinegar and lemon for aioli.
Serve fish and chips with malt vinegar aioli and lemon wedges.
40: Thaw and dice mango. Toss slaw mix with lime juice, minced jalapeño, and a pinch of salt.
Season shrimp with chile-cumin rub; sear 1–2 min per side.
Mix sour cream with a squeeze of lime.
Fill tortillas with shrimp, mango slaw, crema, and cilantro.
41: Dice onion and poblano; cook in oil 5 min until soft.
Add diced chicken and chile-cumin rub; cook 5 min.
Stir in cream base, broth concentrate, corn, and 2 cups water; simmer 10 min.
Finish with lime juice and season to taste.
Top bowls with mozzarella and tortilla strips.
42: Grill or sear the pineapple slice until caramelized.
Form beef into 2 patties; season with chipotle seasoning and garlic salt. Sear 3–4 min per side, melt cheddar on top.
Build burgers with pineapple, onion, and lettuce.
43: Thinly slice cucumber; toss with slaw mix, rice vinegar, and salt for pickled slaw.
Mix mayo with sriracha and hoisin.
Season pork with a pinch of salt; sear 3–4 min per side, slice.
Fill with pork, pickled slaw, jalapeño, cilantro, and the sauce.
44: Sear steak strips in a hot pan 2 min until browned; remove.
Stir-fry broccoli and garlic 3 min with a splash of water to steam.
Return steak; add garlic sesame sauce and toss to glaze.
Serve over rice; top with green onion and sesame seeds.
45: Cook linguine in salted water until al dente.
Halve tomatoes and roast or blister in a hot pan.
Sear scallops seasoned with salt 1–2 min per side until golden; remove.
Wilt spinach; add cream base, parmesan, and lemon to make sauce.
Toss linguine and tomatoes in sauce; top with scallops and red pepper flakes.
46: Butter French bread, sprinkle with garlic salt; toast until crisp.
Wilt spinach with garlic; stir in cream base and parmesan for creamed spinach.
Season sirloin with steak seasoning and garlic salt.
Sear steak 3–4 min per side to desired doneness; rest.
Plate steak with creamed spinach and garlic bread; finish with red pepper flakes.
47: Toss slaw mix with a spoon of mayo and a pinch of salt.
Season chicken with garlic salt; sear 4–5 min per side until cooked through.
Brush chicken with sweet chili sauce to glaze.
Build sandwiches with chicken, slaw, and pickle chips.
48: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil, roast 25–30 min.
Thinly slice onion; caramelize slowly in butter 12–15 min.
Season steak strips with steak seasoning; sear 2–3 min.
Split hoagie rolls; layer steak, onions, and smoked gouda. Broil or press until cheese melts.
Spread rolls with mayo; serve melts with fries.
49: Thinly slice cucumber and green onion; toss with rice vinegar and a pinch of sugar for salad.
Coat chicken thighs in gochujang, sesame oil, garlic, and sugar.
Sear thighs in a hot dry pan 5–6 min per side until caramelized and cooked through.
Serve over rice with cucumber salad; sprinkle sesame seeds.
50: Steam or roast broccoli until tender-crisp.
Sear pork chops seasoned with salt 3–4 min per side; rest and slice.
Simmer teriyaki glaze with orange concentrate and garlic until syrupy; toss pork to coat.
Serve pork over rice with broccoli; top with green onion and sesame seeds.
51: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add asparagus last 12 min.
Season sirloin with steak seasoning and garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side, rest.
Sauté shallot in butter; add cream base and whole grain mustard to make sauce.
Plate steak with mustard cream, potatoes, and asparagus; top with chives.
52: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min.
Halve tomatoes. Season pork chops with garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side, remove.
Sauté shallot; add tomatoes, cream base, herb butter, broth concentrate, and Italian seasoning.
Simmer sauce until thickened; return pork to warm through.
Plate pork with tomato cream sauce and potatoes.
53: Preheat oven to 400°F. Slice bell pepper, toss with oil, roast 15 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt, smoked paprika, and hot sauce.
Roast chicken on the sheet pan 18–20 min to 165°F; top with pepper jack to melt.
Build sandwiches with chicken, roasted peppers, and lettuce.
54: Dice onion and poblano; cook in oil 5 min.
Add diced chicken and chile-cumin rub; cook 5 min.
Stir in cream base, broth concentrate, corn, and 2 cups water; simmer 10 min.
Finish with lime; season to taste.
Top with mozzarella and tortilla strips.
55: Dice onion and tomato; sauté onion 5 min.
Add beef, taco seasoning, and chipotle seasoning; cook until browned.
Add tomato, corn, broth concentrate, and 3 cups water; simmer 12 min.
Ladle into bowls; top with cheese, sour cream, and tortilla strips.
56: Preheat oven to 400°F. Trim green beans, toss with oil, roast 15 min.
Flake salmon; mix with panko, half the mayo, dijon, old bay, and chopped spinach. Form into cakes.
Sear cakes in oil 3–4 min per side until golden.
Mix remaining mayo with capers and lemon for remoulade.
Serve cakes with remoulade, green beans, and lemon.
57: Dice tomato; mix with lime juice and cilantro for pico.
Season chicken with garlic salt; cook 6–7 min, then chop.
Mix cream cheese with wilted spinach.
Fill tortillas with the spinach cream, chicken, and mozzarella; fold.
Cook quesadillas 2 min per side until crisp; serve with pico.
58: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min.
Sauté garlic and sun-dried tomatoes; wilt spinach. Add cream base and parmesan for sauce.
Season sirloin with steak seasoning and garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side, rest.
Simmer sauce until thickened; add red pepper flakes.
Plate steak smothered in Sicilian spinach cream with potatoes.
59: Thaw and dice mango; mix with lime and a pinch of salt for salsa.
Toss shredded cabbage with lime juice.
Season shrimp with chile-cumin rub and sriracha; sear 1–2 min per side.
Crisp tortillas in a dry pan.
Top tostadas with cabbage, shrimp, mango salsa, sour cream, and cilantro.
60: Toss slaw mix with a little sour cream and lime for slaw.
Coat shrimp in panko; fry in shallow oil until golden, ~2 min per side.
Mix remaining sour cream with chipotle seasoning for crema.
Fill with fried shrimp, slaw, and chipotle crema.
61: Cook campanelle in salted water until al dente; reserve a little pasta water.
Halve tomatoes. Brown sausage, breaking apart, 6–8 min.
Add garlic, chickpeas, and tomatoes; cook 3 min. Wilt in spinach.
Toss pasta with the sausage mixture, parmesan, and Italian seasoning, loosening with pasta water.
Finish with red pepper flakes and more parmesan.
62: Preheat oven to 400°F. Cut carrots and zucchini; toss with oil, roast 20–25 min.
Season chicken with herbes de Provence and garlic salt; sear 4 min per side, finish in oven to 165°F.
Sauté shallot; add cream base, broth concentrate, and herb butter for sauce.
Plate chicken with sauce and roasted vegetables; top with chives.
63: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil, roast 25–30 min.
Form beef into 2 patties, season with garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side, add a pat of compound butter and cheddar to melt.
Build burgers with bacon, lettuce, and onion; serve with fries.
64: Preheat oven to 400°F. Thinly slice onion; caramelize in oil 12–15 min.
Season steak strips with garlic salt; sear 2–3 min.
Top flatbreads with cheddar-jack, steak, and onions.
Bake 8–10 min until crisp and melty.
Drizzle with BBQ sauce and ranch; finish with cilantro.
65: Warm pulled pork with chipotle seasoning and a spoon of BBQ sauce.
Fill tortillas with pork and cheddar-jack; roll tightly into flautas.
Cook flautas seam-side down in oil, turning until crisp.
Serve with salsa, sour cream, and cilantro.
66: Cook diced chicken with a pinch of salt until done; cool slightly.
Mix chicken and celery with mayo, old bay, lemon juice, and dill.
Season the chicken salad to taste.
67: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss broccoli with oil, roast 18–20 min. Cook rice per package.
Season chicken with garlic salt and smoked paprika; coat in panko.
Sear chicken in shallow oil until golden, then bake to 165°F.
Warm hot honey with a pat of butter.
Drizzle chicken with hot honey; serve with rice and broccoli.
68: Trim snap peas; stir-fry with garlic 2 min.
Add sweet chili sauce and soy sauce; toss shrimp and peas to glaze.
Serve over rice; top with green onion and sesame seeds.
69: Grill or sear pineapple until caramelized.
Toss slaw mix with a little mayo.
Form beef into 2 patties, season with garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side. Brush with teriyaki.
Build burgers with pineapple and slaw.
70: Dice onion and poblano; cook in oil 5 min.
Add diced chicken and chile-cumin rub; cook 5 min.
Stir in cream base, broth concentrate, corn, and 2 cups water; simmer 10 min.
Finish with lime; season to taste.
Top with mozzarella and tortilla strips.
71: Preheat oven to 400°F. Slice bell pepper and red onion.
Top with mozzarella, pepperoni, peppers, and onion.
Bake 8–10 min until crisp and bubbly.
72: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add green beans last 12 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt; sear 4 min per side, finish in oven to 165°F.
Cook butter with shallot until browned and nutty; add thyme and lemon.
Serve with potatoes and green beans; top with chives.
73: Coat shrimp in panko; fry in shallow oil until crisp.
Toss shrimp and peas with garlic sesame sauce and red pepper flakes.
Serve over rice; top with green onion and sesame seeds.
74: Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil potatoes; mash with herb butter and a splash of cream base.
Roast or steam green beans until tender.
Season chicken with herbes de Provence and garlic salt; sear 4 min per side, finish to 165°F.
Sauté shallot; add cream base and broth concentrate for sauce.
Plate chicken with sauce, mashed potatoes, and green beans; top with chives.
75: Grill or sear pineapple until caramelized.
Toss slaw mix with a little mayo.
Form beef into 2 patties, season with garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side. Brush with teriyaki.
Build burgers with pineapple and slaw.
76: Mix beef with a spoon of garlic sesame sauce; form into meatballs.
Sear meatballs, turning, until browned and cooked through.
Steam or stir-fry broccoli. Simmer remaining sauce with garlic and ginger; toss meatballs to glaze.
Serve over rice with broccoli; top with green onion and sesame seeds.
77: Cook rice per package. Warm black beans.
Coat chicken thighs in jerk seasoning and garlic salt.
Sear thighs 5–6 min per side until caramelized and cooked through; slice.
Build bowls with rice, beans, chicken, and mango; finish with lime and green onion.
78: Toss slaw mix with a little mayo and lemon.
Coat yellowtail in panko seasoned with old bay and garlic salt.
Fry fish in shallow oil 3–4 min per side until golden and flaky.
Toast buns; spread with tartar sauce.
Build sandwiches with fish and slaw; serve with lemon.
79: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add green beans last 12 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt and smoked paprika; coat in panko.
Sear chicken in shallow oil 4–5 min per side until golden.
Mix mayo with lemon for sauce.
Serve schnitzel with potatoes, green beans, and lemon.
80: Thaw and dice mango; toss with slaw mix and lime for mango slaw.
Season shrimp with chipotle seasoning; sear 1–2 min per side.
Mix sour cream with lime for crema.
Fill with shrimp, mango slaw, crema, and cilantro.
81: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil and garlic salt, roast 25–30 min.
Thinly slice onion; caramelize in oil 12–15 min.
Season steak with steak seasoning; sear 3–4 min per side, rest and slice.
Split hoagie rolls; spread with mayo and fig jam. Add steak, onions, and swiss; broil to melt.
Top with arugula; serve with fries.
82: Halve tomatoes. Toast arborio rice in herb butter 1 min.
Add broth concentrate dissolved in 3 cups hot water, one ladle at a time, stirring until absorbed (~20 min).
Sear shrimp with garlic and red pepper flakes 1–2 min per side; remove.
Stir spinach, tomatoes, and parmesan into the risotto.
Fold in shrimp; finish with basil.
83: Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil salted water for spaghetti.
Season chicken with garlic salt; dip in egg, coat in panko with Italian seasoning. Sear until golden.
Top chicken with marinara and mozzarella; bake 8–10 min to 165°F.
Cook spaghetti; toss with a spoon of marinara and garlic.
Plate spaghetti and chicken; top with parmesan.
84: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add broccoli last 15 min.
Mix cream cheese and cheddar. Make crosswise cuts across each chicken breast (hasselback) and fill with cheese.
Season chicken with garlic salt and smoked paprika; bake 20–25 min to 165°F.
Drizzle with lemon garlic butter and parmesan.
Serve with roasted potatoes and broccoli.
85: Dice onion and tomato; sauté onion 5 min.
Add pork, taco seasoning, and chile-cumin rub; cook until browned.
Add tomato, corn, broth concentrate, and 2 cups water; simmer 12 min.
Ladle into bowls; top with cheese and sour cream.
86: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add green beans last 12 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt and smoked paprika; coat in panko.
Sear chicken in shallow oil 4–5 min per side until golden.
Mix mayo with lemon and butter for sauce.
Serve schnitzel with potatoes, green beans, and lemon.
87: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add asparagus last 12 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt; sear 4 min per side, finish in oven to 165°F.
Toast pine nuts in butter until golden; add garlic and lemon.
Spoon pine nut butter over chicken.
Serve with potatoes and asparagus; top with chives.
88: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toast couscous in oil, add 1.5 cups water and broth concentrate, simmer 12 min.
Dice zucchini and tomato; roast with oil 15 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt and za'atar; spread with hummus and press on parmesan. Bake to 165°F.
Fluff couscous with lemon and parsley.
Serve chicken over couscous with roasted vegetables.
89: Preheat oven to 400°F. Slice bell pepper and red onion.
Spread pesto on flatbreads; top with mozzarella, sausage, peppers, and onion.
Bake 8–10 min until crisp and bubbly.
90: Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil potatoes; mash with butter and a splash of cream sauce base.
Layer ham and swiss on chicken; dip in egg, coat in panko. Sear until golden, bake to 165°F.
Warm cream sauce base with a pinch of smoked paprika for cordon bleu sauce.
Plate chicken with sauce, mashed potatoes, and broccoli.
91: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cook penne in salted water until al dente.
Halve tomatoes; roast with oil 15 min.
Spread pesto on chicken, press on panko mixed with parmesan. Sear, then bake to 165°F; top with mozzarella to melt.
Toss penne with tomatoes, garlic, and Italian seasoning.
92: Season turkey with taco seasoning; press thin in a hot pan and smash-cook until browned and crisp.
Layer turkey onto tortillas; top with cheese to melt.
Finish tacos with tomato, sour cream, lime, and cilantro.
93: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add green beans last 12 min.
Sear fish in oil 3–4 min per side until flaky.
Melt lemon garlic butter with parsley and garlic.
Spoon lemon-parsley butter over fish; serve with potatoes and green beans.
94: Preheat oven to 400°F. Peel and cut carrots; toss with oil and roast 25 min, then glaze with honey.
Season pork chops with salt and pepper.
Sear pork 3–4 min per side to 145°F; rest.
Chop smoked almonds; mix with blue cheese.
Top pork with blue cheese and almonds; serve with glazed carrots.
95: Dice onion and potatoes; sauté onion and garlic 5 min.
Add potatoes, broth concentrate, and 3 cups water; simmer 12 min until potatoes are tender.
Stir in cream base and kale; simmer 5 min until wilted.
Season with red pepper flakes and Italian seasoning.
96: Preheat oven to 425°F. Boil potatoes; mash with butter and a splash of cream sauce base.
Layer ham and swiss on chicken; dip in egg, coat in panko. Sear, bake to 165°F.
Warm cream sauce base with smoked paprika for sauce.
Plate chicken with sauce, mashed potatoes, and broccoli.
97: Preheat oven to 400°F. Dice zucchini and tomato.
Toast couscous in oil; add 1.5 cups water and broth concentrate.
Season chicken with garlic salt; add to the pan with vegetables and lemon garlic butter.
Cover and simmer 15 min until couscous is tender and chicken reaches 165°F.
98: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min.
Steam or roast broccoli until tender; chop.
Mix cream cheese, cheddar, and broccoli. Cut a pocket in each chicken breast and stuff.
Season chicken with garlic salt and smoked paprika; sear, then bake to 165°F.
Drizzle with lemon garlic butter and parmesan; serve with potatoes.
99: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil, roast 25–30 min.
Form beef into 2 patties; season with smoky peppercorn seasoning and garlic salt.
Sear patties 3–4 min per side; melt cheddar on top.
Thinly slice onion; toast buns and spread with mayo.
Build burgers with lettuce and onion; serve with fries.
100: Cook linguine in salted water until al dente; reserve pasta water.
Butter French bread, sprinkle with garlic salt; toast.
Sauté garlic in butter; add white wine and lemon, simmer 1 min.
Sear shrimp with red pepper flakes 1–2 min per side.
Toss linguine and shrimp in the scampi sauce with parsley; serve with garlic bread.
101: Warm pulled pork with chipotle seasoning.
Crisp tortillas in a dry pan into tostada shells.
Layer tostadas with pork and nacho cheese; melt briefly.
Top with salsa verde, sour cream, and cilantro.
102: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice zucchini and halve tomatoes; toss with oil.
Make crosswise cuts across each chicken breast (hasselback) and tuck in feta.
Season chicken with garlic salt, Italian seasoning, and herb butter.
Bake chicken and vegetables 25–30 min to 165°F.
103: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add asparagus last 12 min.
Season chicken with herbes de Provence and garlic salt; sear 4 min per side, finish to 165°F.
Sauté shallot; add cream base and herb butter for sauce.
Plate chicken with sauce, potatoes, and asparagus; top with chives.
104: Cook rice per package. Char corn in a dry pan.
Dice celery. Season diced chicken with garlic salt; cook 6–7 min until done.
Build bowls with rice, corn, and buffalo chicken.
Top with cheddar, blue cheese, celery, and green onion.
105: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add asparagus last 12 min.
Spread pesto on salmon; press on panko mixed with parmesan.
Bake salmon 12–15 min until it flakes and the crust is golden.
Serve with roasted potatoes and asparagus.
106: Cook rice per package. Steam edamame.
Fry pork in shallow oil 3–4 min per side until golden and cooked to 145°F; slice.
Serve over rice with edamame; top with green onion and sesame seeds.
107: Dice onion and potatoes; sauté onion and garlic 5 min.
Add potatoes, broth concentrate, and 3 cups water; simmer 12 min.
Stir in cream base and kale; simmer 5 min.
Season with red pepper flakes and Italian seasoning.
108: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil, roast 25–30 min.
Form beef into 2 patties, season with garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side, melt cheddar.
Toast buns; spread with mayo and smoky dipping sauce.
Build burgers with lettuce and crispy onions.
Serve with fries and extra dipping sauce.
109: Preheat oven to 425°F. Roast broccoli 18–20 min. Cook spaghetti until al dente.
Cut chicken into nugget pieces; coat in panko seasoned with garlic salt. Sear in oil until golden and cooked through.
Sauté garlic in butter; add cream base and parmesan for alfredo.
Toss spaghetti in the alfredo with Italian seasoning.
Serve nuggets over pasta with broccoli.
110: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add green beans last 12 min.
Season chicken with garlic salt and smoked paprika; coat in panko.
Sear chicken in shallow oil 4–5 min per side until golden.
Mix mayo with lemon for sauce.
Serve schnitzel with potatoes, green beans, and lemon.
111: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil, roast 25–30 min.
Form beef into 2 patties; season with smoky peppercorn seasoning and garlic salt.
Sear patties 3–4 min per side; melt cheddar.
Thinly slice onion; toast buns and spread with mayo.
Build burgers with lettuce and onion; serve with fries.
112: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add green beans last 12 min.
Thinly slice apple and shallot; sauté in butter with brown sugar until caramelized.
Season pork chops with salt and thyme; sear 3–4 min per side to 145°F.
Add broth concentrate and mustard to the apples; simmer into a sauce.
Spoon apple-onion sauce over pork; serve with potatoes and green beans.
113: Dice onion and poblano; cook in oil 5 min.
Add diced chicken and chile-cumin rub; cook 5 min.
Stir in cream base, broth concentrate, corn, and 2 cups water; simmer 10 min.
Finish with lime; season to taste.
Top with mozzarella and tortilla strips.
114: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cut potatoes into fries, toss with oil, roast 25–30 min.
Cook bacon with a sprinkle of brown sugar until candied and crisp.
Form beef into 2 patties, season with garlic salt; sear 3–4 min per side, melt smoked gouda.
Thinly slice onion; toast buns and spread with mayo.
Build burgers with candied bacon, lettuce, and onion; serve with fries.
115: Steam or roast broccoli until tender.
Sear pork chops seasoned with salt 3–4 min per side; rest and slice.
Simmer garlic sesame sauce with orange concentrate and garlic; toss pork to glaze.
Serve over rice with broccoli; top with green onion and sesame seeds.
116: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add tomatoes last 12 min.
Cut a pocket in each chicken breast; stuff with spinach and feta.
Season chicken with garlic salt, Italian seasoning, and za'atar; sear, then bake to 165°F.
Squeeze lemon over; serve with potatoes and tomatoes.
117: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min. Add asparagus last 12 min.
Sear salmon skin-side down 4 min, flip, cook 3 more min until it flakes.
Melt lemon garlic butter with capers, garlic, lemon, and parsley for piccata butter.
Spoon piccata butter over salmon; serve with potatoes and asparagus.
118: Halve tomato. Toast arborio rice 1 min.
Add beef demi-glace dissolved in 3 cups hot water, one ladle at a time, stirring until absorbed (~20 min).
Season steak strips with pot roast seasoning; sear 2–3 min.
Stir corn, tomato, parmesan, and sour cream into the risotto.
Fold in steak; top with green onion.
119: Preheat oven to 400°F. Peel and cut carrots; toss with oil, roast 25 min, then glaze with honey.
Season pork chops with salt and pepper.
Sear pork 3–4 min per side to 145°F; rest.
Chop smoked almonds; mix with blue cheese.
Top pork with blue cheese and almonds; serve with glazed carrots.
120: Dice onion and poblano; cook in oil 5 min.
Add diced chicken and chile-cumin rub; cook 5 min.
Stir in cream base, broth concentrate, corn, and 2 cups water; simmer 10 min.
Finish with lime; season to taste.
Top with mozzarella and tortilla strips.
121: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes, toss with oil, roast 25 min.
Slice mushrooms; sauté with garlic until golden.
Season diced chicken with rotisserie seasoning and garlic salt; cook 6–7 min.
Add cream base and herb butter; simmer into a sauce with the mushrooms.
Fold chicken through; top with parmesan and green onion. Serve with potatoes.
122: Preheat oven to 425°F. Dice potatoes and slice bell pepper; toss with oil and garlic pepper, roast 15 min.
Lay trout on the sheet pan; brush with lemon garlic butter and Sicilian sauce.
Return pan to oven; roast 10–12 min until fish flakes.
Sprinkle parmesan over everything and roast 2 more min.
Serve trout with potatoes and peppers.
123: Cook fettuccine in salted water until al dente; reserve pasta water.
Dice zucchini; sauté with garlic 4 min.
Season scallops with blackening seasoning; sear 1–2 min per side until golden; remove.
Add cream base, lemon, and parmesan to the pan; simmer into a sauce.
Toss fettuccine and zucchini in sauce; top with scallops and chives.
